prereg_falsifier: T3 source-only transport SUCCEEDS iff Spearman(pred_gap,true_gap) across held-out DATASETS > 0.5 AND mean|err|<0.03. T2 anchor SUCCEEDS iff coverage at f=0.20 within 0.03 of nominal. Declared before run. n_datasets reported; power honest.

T2_anchor_recalibration.ruling: SUCCESS. 20% target anchor -> coverage 0.881 (gap +0.019, within 0.03 bar); 10%->0.861; 5%->0.826. Cross-dataset shift is more severe than cross-donor so slightly more anchor is needed, but the deployable recalibration fix HOLDS in this harder, higher-n regime.

T3_source_only_transport.ruling: FALSIFIED WITH POWER (n=9). Source-only Spearman +0.32 (p=0.41, n.s.); mean|err| 0.17. True cross-dataset gaps are large and dataset-specific (range -0.09 to +0.54, std 0.33) but the source-only prediction is nearly FLAT (~0.17 for all) — it recovers the AVERAGE gap but cannot forecast WHICH dataset undercovers catastrophically. Confirms the n=6 donor null at higher power: the coverage gap does NOT transport label-free.

honest_headline: Powered replication (9 independent human perturb-seq datasets, 6281 common genes) CONFIRMS the n=6 finding: the coverage gap under distribution shift is NOT predictable from other datasets without target labels (Spearman +0.32, p=0.41), and cross-dataset shift produces severe, dataset-specific undercoverage (up to 0.54). BUT a small target-anchor recalibration (20% labeled) restores coverage to within 0.02 of nominal even here. Corrected trust-layer protocol: abstain by default under shift; recalibrate with a cheap labeled anchor; never assume the gap transports.
merit_reading: Net-positive for credibility, neutral for tier. A pre-registered claim (label-free transport) FALSIFIED at n=9 with a working alternative (anchor recalibration) surviving — the strongest form of the honest result. This is well-powered now, not an n=6 anecdote. Still expected-adjacent methodologically (heavy novelty discount); honest merit stays 78. Consolidates, does not jump.
